text: The relationship between blood sodium plasma concentrations and blood plasma volume has been studied in relation to movement associated with osmotic gradients, as well as active transport using adenosine triphosphate to move sodium across membranes against its electrochemical gradient.  Through toxicant and electrophysical studies, evidence shows that decreases in blood sodium plasma concentrations lead to decrease in blood plasma volume.  Hematocrit values are often used as an indirect measure of blood plasma volume based on the volume of red blood cells in total blood volume (Gordon et al. 2003).
